message:
feat(spec-explorer,components,e2e): add deep-linkable URL state, fix hydration mismatches, and expand mobile regression coverage

Three interconnected improvements ship together because they share the
same hydration-safety foundation and were validated as a unit:

1. Spec Explorer deep-link URL state and interactive section rail

   The spec viewer previously had no URL persistence — opening a doc,
   selecting a category, searching, or scrolling to a section was all
   ephemeral state that vanished on reload or link-sharing. This adds
   full query-param-driven state (`?doc=`, `?category=`, `?q=`,
   `#section-id`) with proper browser history integration:

   - `parseViewerUrlState` / `buildViewerRelativeUrl` provide a
     deterministic round-trip between URL and component state.
   - `popstate` listener restores state on back/forward navigation.
   - `historyModeRef` tracks whether the next URL update should push
     (user-initiated navigation) or replace (filter/scroll changes).
   - `suppressUrlSyncRef` prevents feedback loops when popstate fires.
   - Reader progress is now tracked via scroll position and displayed
     as a gradient progress bar and percentage in both the overview
     header and the desktop section rail.
   - The section rail gains animated dot indicators and a layoutId
     spring animation for the active section highlight.
   - Related-doc buttons now carry `data-spec-related-doc` attributes
     for targeted e2e assertions.

2. Hydration mismatch fixes across motion components

   Several components imported `useReducedMotion` directly from
   framer-motion, which returns `null` on the server and a boolean on
   the client, causing React hydration mismatches on every SSR page
   load. The fix centralizes all reduced-motion access through
   `@/components/motion`, which wraps the hook with
   `useSyncExternalStore` to return a stable `false` during SSR and
   the real preference only after hydration. Affected components:

   - animated-number, client-shell, custom-cursor, robot-mascot,
     section-shell, sync-elements, glow-orbits

   GlowOrbits additionally suffered from a two-phase hydration issue:
   its `parallaxReady` state started false on the server but flipped
   to true in a useEffect, causing the motion style object to appear
   mid-render. The fix replaces the state flag with a
   `getInitialPointerValue` helper that provides stable initial values
   for the motion values themselves, removing the conditional style
   branch entirely.

   The hero-media component had a parallel issue: the reduced-motion
   fallback branch rendered a completely different DOM tree (an
   `<Image>` element) vs. the animated branch, guaranteeing a
   mismatch when the server picked one branch and the client the
   other. The fix removes the conditional branch so both server and
   client always render the TUI demo markup — the animation timers
   simply don't fire when reduced motion is active, achieving the
   same graceful degradation without a DOM divergence.

   custom-cursor also dropped its early `return null` on reduced
   motion, which caused the same server/client tree mismatch. The
   cursor element now always renders but the animation hooks already
   no-op when reduced motion is detected.

3. Mobile Playwright regression coverage

   New mobile-only test suite (`visualizations.spec.ts`) adds four
   targeted regression tests that run exclusively in the
   `mobile-chrome` project:

   - Hero media stays within mobile viewport bounds
   - File reservations visualization has no horizontal overflow
   - Flywheel uses bottom-sheet dialog interaction without overflow
   - Home page renders without hydration/runtime console errors

   Each test captures annotated screenshots and asserts
   `scrollWidth <= innerWidth` to catch overflow regressions. A
   shared `collectUnexpectedErrors` helper filters allowlisted noise
   (e.g. missing favicon) from real errors.

   The smoke spec gains a deep-link restoration test that navigates
   to a parameterized URL, verifies doc/category/fragment state,
   clicks a related doc, then uses `page.goBack()` to confirm
   history state is properly restored.

   Beads issue tracker updated to close the mobile rendering
   stabilization epic (bd-ilq) and all child tasks, plus close the
   spec explorer deep-link feature (bd-35g) and its subtasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
